vineyards.name: Domaine de la Romanee-Conti
vineyards.address: 1 Rue Derriere le Four, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.winemaker: Bertrand de Villaine and Perrine Fenal (co-direction)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public tastings or visits. Wines are sold strictly on allocation through approved importers and en-bloc cases.
vineyards.description: The most revered estate in Burgundy, monopole owner of Romanee-Conti and La Tache and farmer of a roughly 28-hectare grand-cru holding in Vosne-Romanee.
vineyards.tip: Allocation only; there is no cellar door. Bottles surface at auction and via a handful of importers.

vineyards.name: Domaine Leroy
vineyards.address: 15 Rue de la Fontaine, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public tastings. The biodynamic domaine sells on tight allocation through importers.
vineyards.description: Lalou Bize-Leroy's biodynamic Vosne-Romanee domaine, built from 1988 on the former Noellat and Remy vines, now among the most sought-after and expensive wines in Burgundy.
vineyards.tip: Demeter-certified and allocation-only; no visits. Look for the rare Domaine Leroy versus the separate Maison Leroy negociant label.

vineyards.name: Domaine Meo-Camuzet
vineyards.address: 11 Rue des Grands Crus, 21700 Vosne-Romanee, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment only; the domaine also runs a Meo-Camuzet Frere et Soeurs negociant range.
vineyards.description: Vosne-Romanee domaine shaped by Henri Jayer's influence, holding Richebourg, Clos de Vougeot and Vosne premier crus under Jean-Nicolas Meo.

vineyards.name: Domaine Mugneret-Gibourg
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public cellar door; wines sold on allocation.
vineyards.description: A family domaine run by the Mugneret sisters in Vosne-Romanee, prized for Echezeaux, Ruchottes-Chambertin and Clos de Vougeot of great purity.

vineyards.name: Domaine Armand Rousseau
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public tastings; the domaine sells on allocation through importers.
vineyards.description: The reference domaine of Gevrey-Chambertin, with Chambertin and Chambertin-Clos de Beze leading a holding of muscular, age-worthy grand-cru reds.

vineyards.name: Domaine Trapet Pere et Fils
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment; the domaine farms biodynamically.
vineyards.description: Long-established Gevrey-Chambertin domaine, biodynamic since the 1990s, with holdings in Chambertin, Latricieres-Chambertin and Chapelle-Chambertin.

vineyards.name: Domaine Georges Roumier
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public cellar door; allocation-only through importers.
vineyards.description: The benchmark Chambolle-Musigny domaine under Christophe Roumier, famed for Bonnes-Mares, Musigny and the rare monopole Clos de la Bussiere.

vineyards.name: Domaine Comte Georges de Vogue
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.owner: de Causans and de Ladoucette family
vineyards.tasting_program: No public tastings; sold through the exclusive UK agent and importers.
vineyards.description: The historic Chambolle-Musigny domaine and largest owner of the Musigny Grand Cru, tracing its line in the village back more than five centuries.

vineyards.name: Domaine Dujac
vineyards.address: 7 Rue de la Bussiere, 21220 Morey-Saint-Denis, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.winemaker: Jeremy Seysses and Diana Snowden Seysses
vineyards.tasting_program: No public cellar door; allocation through importers.
vineyards.description: Morey-Saint-Denis domaine founded by Jacques Seysses, known for whole-cluster Clos de la Roche, Clos Saint-Denis and Chambertin under the next generation.

vineyards.name: Domaine Ponsot
vineyards.address: 21 Rue de la Montagne, 21220 Gilly-les-Citeaux, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings at the Gilly-les-Citeaux cellar by appointment.
vineyards.description: Morey-Saint-Denis domaine famed for the monopole Clos de la Roche Cuvee Vieilles Vignes and its long-lived, low-sulphur reds.

vineyards.name: Domaine de la Vougeraie
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment; biodynamic estate.
vineyards.description: Biodynamic domaine assembled by the Boisset family across the Cote de Nuits and Cote de Beaune, with the Clos Blanc de Vougeot monopole among its holdings.

vineyards.name: Domaine Faiveley
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Nuits, Cote de Beaune and Cote Chalonnaise, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment at the Nuits-Saint-Georges cellars.
vineyards.description: Major family domaine and negociant based in Nuits-Saint-Georges, one of Burgundy's largest grand-cru owners across the Cote de Nuits and Cote Chalonnaise.

vineyards.name: Maison Albert Bichot
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Burgundy, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment; the house owns six estate domaines across Burgundy.
vineyards.description: Beaune-based family negociant and estate owner, with domaines including Clos Frantin in Vosne-Romanee and Long-Depaquit in Chablis.

vineyards.name: Maison Louis Jadot
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Burgundy, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: The Beaune cellars open for tastings and bottle purchase; afternoons on weekdays and Saturdays.
vineyards.description: Major Beaune negociant and domaine owner since 1859, holding premier and grand crus across the Cote d'Or plus Beaujolais cru estates.

vineyards.name: Maison Joseph Drouhin
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Burgundy, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours of the historic cellars beneath Beaune and a six-wine tasting by appointment.
vineyards.description: Family negociant and domaine in the heart of Beaune, biodynamic across its estate vines, with cellars under the old Parlement de Bourgogne.

vineyards.name: Bouchard Pere et Fils
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune and Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and cellar visits at the Chateau de Beaune by appointment.
vineyards.description: One of Burgundy's oldest houses, based at the Chateau de Beaune, with the Montrachet, Chevalier-Montrachet and Le Corton grand crus among its large holding.

vineyards.name: Bouchard Aine et Fils
vineyards.classification: Premier Cru and Village (Burgundy, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Sensory tasting experiences and cellar visits at the Beaune mansion, open to walk-ins and bookings.
vineyards.description: Beaune negociant house founded in 1750, now part of the Boisset group, known for an interactive five-senses tasting tour in its town-centre cellars.

vineyards.name: Maison Chanson
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings inside the 15th-century Bastion fortress cellars by appointment.
vineyards.description: Beaune house owned by the Bollinger family, cellaring its wines in a circular 15th-century rampart bastion, with strong Beaune and Savigny premier crus.

vineyards.name: Maison Patriarche Pere et Fils
vineyards.classification: Premier Cru and Village (Burgundy, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Self-guided tour of five kilometres of historic cellars with a tasting of multiple Burgundy wines, open daily.
vineyards.description: Beaune's largest cellars, housed in a former convent, offering one of the region's best-known self-guided cellar tours and tastings beneath the town.

vineyards.name: Maison Louis Latour
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and tours by appointment; the house is the largest owner of Corton-Charlemagne grand cru vines.
vineyards.description: Beaune family house in business since 1797, the largest holder of Corton-Charlemagne, with a reputation built on grand-cru whites of the Cote de Beaune.

vineyards.name: Domaine Leflaive
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No general cellar door; the family also runs the Olivier Leflaive table and tastings nearby in the village.
vineyards.description: The reference white-Burgundy domaine, biodynamic since the 1990s, with prized parcels of Montrachet, Chevalier-Montrachet and Batard-Montrachet in Puligny.

vineyards.name: Olivier Leflaive Freres
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided tasting lunches and flights at the village table and a small estate hotel; open to bookings.
vineyards.description: Puligny-Montrachet negociant and domaine founded by Olivier Leflaive, best known for its sit-down tasting table pairing flights of white Burgundy with food.

vineyards.name: Domaine Etienne Sauzet
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No general cellar door; allocation through importers.
vineyards.description: Puligny-Montrachet domaine of crystalline whites, biodynamic, with parcels in Batard-Montrachet, Bienvenues-Batard and a roll of Puligny premier crus.

vineyards.name: Domaine des Comtes Lafon
vineyards.address: Clos de la Barre, 21190 Meursault, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No general cellar door; sold on allocation. The family also runs Heritiers du Comte Lafon in the Maconnais.
vineyards.description: The leading Meursault domaine, biodynamic under Dominique Lafon, prized for Meursault Perrieres and Genevrieres and a sliver of Le Montrachet.

vineyards.name: Domaine Coche-Dury
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public tastings; among the most allocation-tight and collected whites in Meursault.
vineyards.description: Cult Meursault domaine under Raphael Coche, famed for flinty, reductive Meursault and a tiny production of Corton-Charlemagne grand cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Ramonet
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No general cellar door; allocation through importers.
vineyards.description: Chassagne-Montrachet domaine of benchmark whites, with holdings in Le Montrachet, Batard-Montrachet and Bienvenues-Batard plus top Chassagne premier crus.

vineyards.name: Domaine de Montille
vineyards.address: Rue du Pied de la Vallee, 21190 Volnay, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune and Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment at the Volnay estate; biodynamic farming.
vineyards.description: Volnay-based biodynamic domaine known for structured, classically built Volnay and Pommard premier crus and a growing range of whites.

vineyards.name: Domaine du Comte Armand
vineyards.classification: Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment; biodynamic estate.
vineyards.description: Pommard domaine and sole owner of the walled Clos des Epeneaux monopole, a biodynamic estate making dense, age-worthy Pommard.

vineyards.name: Domaine Bonneau du Martray
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment; biodynamic estate centred on the Corton hill.
vineyards.description: A rare almost-monopole grand-cru estate on the Corton hill, making only Corton-Charlemagne white and Corton red from its single biodynamic holding.

vineyards.name: Domaine Rollin Pere et Fils
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment at the Pernand-Vergelesses cellar.
vineyards.description: Family domaine in Pernand-Vergelesses making well-priced Corton-Charlemagne and Pernand whites alongside Aloxe-Corton and Savigny reds.

vineyards.name: Domaine William Fevre
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tasting room in the heart of Chablis open by appointment; the largest grand-cru landowner in Chablis.
vineyards.description: The leading Chablis domaine, owned since 2024 by Domaines Barons de Rothschild, with the largest grand-cru holding in the appellation including Les Clos and Bougros.

vineyards.name: Domaine Raveneau
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru and Premier Cru (Chablis, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: No public cellar door; the most collected Chablis domaine, sold on tight allocation.
vineyards.description: The cult Chablis domaine, famed for ageworthy, oak-fermented Les Clos, Valmur and Blanchot grand crus and premier crus like Montee de Tonnerre.

vineyards.name: Domaine Louis Michel et Fils
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment; the domaine ferments entirely in stainless steel to showcase Chablis minerality.
vineyards.description: Steel-only Chablis domaine making precise, unoaked grand and premier crus including Les Clos, Vaudesir and Montee de Tonnerre.

vineyards.name: Domaine Jean-Marc Brocard
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis and Saint-Bris, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings at the Sainte-Claire estate by Prehy church; large organic and biodynamic holdings.
vineyards.description: Major Chablis grower around Prehy, organic and partly biodynamic, also bottling Saint-Bris Sauvignon and Irancy reds from the Grand Auxerrois.

vineyards.name: Maison J. Moreau et Fils
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and shop in Chablis; a historic house with grand-cru parcels including Les Clos.
vineyards.description: One of the oldest Chablis houses, founded in 1814, now part of the Boisset group, with a long association with the Les Clos grand cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Laroche
vineyards.address: L'Obediencerie, 22 Rue Louis Bro, 89800 Chablis, France
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Chablis, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings in the medieval Obediencerie cellars in central Chablis, open to bookings.
vineyards.description: Chablis house cellared in a ninth-century monastic building, with grand crus including Les Clos, Blanchot and the Reserve de l'Obedience bottling.

vineyards.name: Domaines Devillard
vineyards.classification: Premier Cru and Village (Cote Chalonnaise and Cote de Nuits, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings at Chateau de Chamirey in Mercurey by appointment; the group also owns Domaine des Perdrix in Nuits.
vineyards.description: Cote Chalonnaise family group centred on Chateau de Chamirey in Mercurey, with Mercurey and Rully premier crus and a Nuits-Saint-Georges estate.

vineyards.name: Maison Verget
vineyards.classification: Grand Cru, Premier Cru and Village (Maconnais, Chablis and Cote de Beaune, AOC)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment at Sologny; the negociant arm of Guffens-Heynen sourcing across the Maconnais and Chablis.
vineyards.description: Maconnais negociant founded by Jean-Marie Guffens, sourcing precise, terroir-driven Chardonnay from Pouilly-Fuisse to the Cote de Beaune and Chablis.
